title: Number of Ways to Buy Pens and Pencils
description:
You are given an integer total indicating the amount of money you have. You are also given two integers cost1 and cost2 indicating the price of a pen and pencil respectively. You can spend part or all of your money to buy multiple quantities (or none) of each kind of writing utensil.

Return _the number of distinct ways you can buy some number of pens and pencils._

solutionCode.python:
```python
from collections import defaultdict

def sum_of_intervals(arr):
    n = len(arr)
    indices = defaultdict(list)
    intervals = [0] * n

    for i in range(n):
        indices[arr[i]].append(i)

    for i in range(n):
        for index in indices[arr[i]]:
            intervals[i] += abs(index - i)

    return intervals
```
    
    1. Store the indices of each element in arr in a separate data structure (in this case, a vector for C++, an ArrayList for Java, a defaultdict for Python, and an Array for JavaScript).(indices[arr[i]] contains all the indices where arr[i] appears)
2. For each element in arr, loop through the indices[arr[i]] and sum up the intervals between index i and other indices.
3. Add this sum to intervals[i] and return the intervals array.
